evals.prompt: I want to find verified work emails for 5,000 leads using LeadMagic's API. I have first names, last names, and company domains. How do I set this up and what will it cost?
evals.expected_output: Technical guide covering Email Finder endpoint usage, authentication, credit cost calculation, and plan recommendation

assertions.name: email_finder_endpoint
assertions.description: Must specify the POST /v1/people/email-finder endpoint with X-API-Key authentication and the required parameters (first_name, last_name, company_name)

assertions.name: credit_calculation
assertions.description: Must calculate credit cost: up to 5,000 credits (1 per valid result, 0 for not_found), likely less since failed lookups are free

assertions.name: plan_recommendation
assertions.description: Must recommend an appropriate plan — Essential ($99.99/mo, 10,000 credits) or Growth ($179.99/mo, 20,000 credits) based on the 5,000 lookup volume

evals.prompt: We're evaluating LeadMagic vs Apollo for our enrichment needs. We're a 5-person sales team doing about 3,000 email lookups per month. Which is better for us?
evals.expected_output: Comparison covering pricing models, data quality, features, and recommendation based on team size and volume

assertions.name: pricing_comparison
assertions.description: Must compare LeadMagic's pay-per-result model (3,000 lookups on Essential at ~$100/mo) vs Apollo's per-seat subscription model ($49+/user/mo = $245+/mo for 5 users)

assertions.name: feature_comparison
assertions.description: Must note Apollo is an all-in-one platform (prospecting + enrichment + outbound) while LeadMagic is API-first enrichment only — no built-in outbound sequences

assertions.name: data_quality
assertions.description: Must mention LeadMagic's 97% email accuracy claim and pay-only-for-found model vs Apollo's 275M+ contact database with built-in prospecting

evals.prompt: I want to set up LeadMagic's MCP server in Claude Code so I can do prospect research directly from my terminal. How do I configure it?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step MCP server setup guide for Claude Code with API key configuration

assertions.name: mcp_setup
assertions.description: Must explain installing the MCP server via npx leadmagic-mcp or adding it to Claude Code's MCP configuration

assertions.name: api_key_config
assertions.description: Must explain setting the LEADMAGIC_API_KEY environment variable for authentication

assertions.name: available_tools
assertions.description: Must mention that all 19 LeadMagic tools become available as MCP tools in Claude Code, including email finder, company search, and ads intelligence
